revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Bureau of Shrines]][[ja:神社局]][[zh:神社局]]


{{Short description|Japanese government agency|qq=}}
{{Infobox organization
| formation                = {{start date and age|1900|04|26|qq=}}
| predecessor              = [[Bureau of Shrines and Temples]]
| successor                = {{ill|Institute of Divinities|en|Institute of Divinities|qid=Q11590709|lt=Institute of Divinities|1=Institute of Divinities}}
| dissolved                = {{end date and age|1940|11|09|qq=}}
| parent_organization      = {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Home Ministry|1=Home Ministry}}
}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Bureau of Shrines'''|神社局|Jinjakyoku|qq=}} was an internal department of the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of the Interior|1=Home Ministry}} that existed until {{ill|1940|en|1940|qid=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}}. It was in charge of administrative matters related to {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}}, {{ill|Shinkan (official)|en|Shinkan (official)|qid=Q11589454|lt=Shinkan|1=Shinkan (official)}}, and [[Kannushi]].
It was split off from the [[Bureau of Shrines and Temples]] in 1900<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Glossary of Shinto Names and Terms: S |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/glossary/def_S.html#shaji_kyoku |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Basic Terms of Shinto: J |url=https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/ijcc/wp/bts/bts_j.html#Jinjakyoku |access-date=2023-03-10 |website=www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp|qq=}}</ref> with other religions and [[Sect Shinto]] being covered under the [[Bureau of Religions]].<ref name="www2kokugakuinacjp-2023a" />
On April 26, 1900, under the basic policy of the Meiji Restoration government that "shrines are the state's religious ceremonies," the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}}' [[Bureau of Shrines and Temples|Shrines and Temple Bureau]] was abolished and reorganized into the Bureau of Shrines, which administered {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, and the [[:en:Bureau of Religion|Bureau of Religion]]s, which administered other religions including Buddhism, and [[Sect Shinto]].<ref>{{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|en|Ikuhiko Hata|qid=Q5996291|lt=秦郁彦|1=Ikuhiko Hata}}編『日本官僚制総合事典：1868 - 2000』{{ill|University of Tokyo Press|en|University of Tokyo Press|qid=Q842460|lt=東京大学出版会|1=University of Tokyo Press}}、2001st year 。</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=社寺局｜アジ歴グロッサリー |url=https://www.jacar.go.jp/glossary/term3/0010-0030-0040-0010-0200.html |access-date=2022-06-19 |website=www.jacar.go.jp|qq=}}</ref> In other words, the Ministry of Home Affairs' official regulations were revised, seven bureaus were established in the Ministry, the Bureau of Shrines was added before the Regional Bureaus, and the [[Bureau of Shrines and Temples|Shrines and Temple Bureau]] was renamed the [[:en:Bureau of Religion|Bureau of Religion]]. The Bureau of Shrines was criticized by the [[Jōdo Shinshū|Jodo Shinshu]] sect, but the Bureau of Shrines itself was not a very large organization.
It was staffed by one director, one secretary, one archivist, two engineers, one clerk, two assistant archivists, and others.
It was abolished on November 9, 1940 (Showa 15), with the establishment of the {{ill|Institute of Divinities|en|Institute of Divinities|qid=Q11590709|lt=Institute of Divinities|1=Institute of Divinities}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{ill|Ikuhiko Hata|en|Ikuhiko Hata|qid=Q5996291|lt=秦郁彦|1=Ikuhiko Hata}}編『日本官僚制総合事典：1868 - 2000』{{ill|University of Tokyo Press|en|University of Tokyo Press|qid=Q842460|lt=東京大学出版会|1=University of Tokyo Press}}、2001st year 。
== See also ==
* [[Department of Divinities]]
* [[Ministry of Religion (Japan)]]
* {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}
* [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]
